concept.title: AI-Ready Content Authoring Guide
concept.shortdesc: Guidelines for writing DITA content that is optimized for the Extense chatbot, RAG retrieval, and AI-driven content delivery.

section.title: Why AI-ready content matters
section.p: The Extense chatbot uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to answer user questions. It works by:

ol.li: Chunking your DITA content into sections
Embedding each chunk as a vector in a database
Retrieving the most relevant chunks for a user's question
Generating an answer grounded in those chunks

section.p: The quality of the chatbot's answers depends directly on the quality of your content. Well-structured, well-metadata'd DITA content produces better retrieval and better answers.

section.title: Required prolog metadata
section.p: Every topic must include these elements in the prolog for chatbot ingestion:

row.entry: Used as chunk summary in retrieval results
One clear sentence describing the topic's purpose
Routes questions to the right content area
Matches user intent to content type
Links content to Extense service offerings

section.title: Writing for retrieval quality
section.p: These authoring practices improve chatbot answer quality:

ol.li: Write clear shortdescs. The shortdesc is prepended to every chunk from that topic. A vague shortdesc degrades every chunk.
Use section titles as questions. Instead of "Publishing Overview", use "How does DITA publishing work?" — this matches how users ask the chatbot.
Keep sections focused. One section = one concept. The chunking pipeline splits at section boundaries.
Front-load key information. The first 1-2 sentences of each section are the most important for retrieval ranking.
Use DITA terms consistently. The glossary is used for term resolution — always use the canonical glossterm form.
Include code examples. Codeblocks with outputclass="language-*" are preserved in chunks and are highly valued in retrieval.
Add indexterms for synonyms. If users might search for "filter" or "conditional processing", include both as indexterms even if the topic title says "DITAVAL."

section.title: Understanding chunking boundaries
section.p: The ingestion pipeline chunks content at section boundaries. Each chunk:

ul.li: Inherits the topic's title, shortdesc, and all prolog metadata
Gets its own section title and section ID
Is sized between 50 and 1500 tokens (large sections are split by paragraph)
Is prefixed with the topic title + section title for embedding context
Give every <section> both an @id and a <title>
Avoid sections that are just a single sentence
Put related information in the same section rather than splitting across multiple

section.title: Contributing to the glossary
section.p: The glossary serves as the chatbot's term resolution engine. When adding terms:

ul.li: Write <glossUsage> as practical guidance, not just definitions
Include <glossScopeNote> for version applicability and context

section.title: Running the ingestion pipeline
section.p: After updating content, regenerate the chatbot data:
section.codeblock: # From the organized-dita-docs directory: python3 dita-topics/intelligent-content/dita-to-chatbot-json.py \ --input dita-topics \ --output dita-topics/intelligent-content/chatbot-data \ --format jsonl

ul.li: chunks.jsonl — Embed into your vector database
content-manifest.json — Topic index for the chatbot navigator
glossary-index.json — Term lookup for the chatbot
